cards.back: The process of transforming or creating new features from existing ones to improve model accuracy and performance.
The process of selecting a subset of relevant features from the existing feature set to improve model performance and reduce overfitting.
The process of reducing the number of features in a dataset while preserving the most important information, making it easier to model and analyze.
A technique used for dimensionality reduction that transforms the original features into a new set of orthogonal features (principal components) that capture the majority of the variance in the data.
A technique used to select the number of principal components by selecting the principal components with the largest variance, which captures the most important information in the data.
